summary_one_line: Winners produce IPv6 packets whose effective next-header field is not kProtoUdp, routing the switch to a non-UDP case.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes that survive IPv6 header parsing and arrive at HandlePayload with aIpProto != kProtoUdp (i.e. resolve to kProtoTcp, kProtoIcmp6, or an unknown protocol that falls into the default branch). Concretely this requires specific magic byte sequences in the protocol/next-header fields that route away from UDP rather than into the extension-header chain that ultimately unwinds to UDP.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds reach the kProtoTcp case and the default arm (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W F=10 at L937, plus W=4 hits on the default ExitNow path at L944); they skip the heavy HopOpts/extension-header processing (HandleOptions W=5 vs L=95) and never engage fragment reassembly (HandleFragment W=7 L=0 means W only reaches that function, but more importantly L never does and L still always lands on kProtoUdp).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds always take the True side of `case kProtoUdp` (L937 T=20 F=0); they pack many extension-header bytes (HandleOptions L=95) and that chain consistently unwinds to next-header == kProtoUdp, so they never explore the false side. Naive/value_profile lack a way to discover the specific protocol-field byte values that route to non-UDP cases.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the switch/CMP entries that test aIpProto against the literal kProto* constants (and the upstream extension-header walk's equality tests on next-header values), and substitutes those exact constants into the input bytes at offsets matching the other operand. Naive and value_profile have no I2S stage and must blindly mutate bytes to hit the right protocol value; without I2S substitution, the only path to discover the right protocol byte is random havoc, which is what keeps both losers blocked for 12h.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `case kProtoUdp:` in HandlePayload's switch on aIpProto and the blocked side is False.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Source line and Globally blocked side

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs are I2S-delta with cmplog/vpc winning 10/10 and naive/vp blocked 10/10.
Winners reach the kProtoTcp case and the default ExitNow arm; losers never do.
Default-arm ExitNow is W-only at line 945.
At the blocker line, W takes the false side 10 times while L never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 937  T=20 F=10  T=20 F=0  case kProtoUdp:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Losers process IPv6 extension-header options 19x more than winners, consistent with chains that unwind to UDP.
Winner seeds are dominated by cmplog discoveries.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=27411878ea3c592d, size=57 bytes, fuzzer=cmplog, trial=1, discovered_at=94s,

evidence_trail.claim: Cmplog's mutator stack includes I2SRandReplace, the only I2S-substitution mutator across the four fuzzers.
Naive's mutator pool contains no I2S substitution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: Value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its only CMP-aware feedback is the CMP_MAP gradient.
Winner inputs show concentrated, near-identical byte sequences across all 5 sampled seeds (e.g. the 10-byte run at offsets 0x14-0x17 is invariant), consistent with I2S substituting a fixed literal.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 8469 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 27411878ea3c592d` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 12 of the 13-level chain (ancestor 4578ba613b6d1a35). Cross-checked with `--seed 195625f507563264` (28-level chain) and observed the same dash-row ancestor 4578ba613b6d1a35 at depth 27 — both winning seeds share a common I2S-floor root. Per fuzzer_mechanism_library.md cmplog section, dash rows are exclusive to cmplog/vpc under the current LibAFL build and indicate an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find (lower bound — some I2S finds leak into the havoc bucket).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness that replaces the kProtoUdp/kProtoTcp/kProtoIcmp6 equality switch with a runtime-derived protocol value (so the comparison constants are not literals visible to CmpLogObserver) still showed cmplog/vpc resolving in <1h while naive/vp stayed blocked, the I2S literal-substitution mechanism would be falsified — the divergence would have to come from somewhere other than I2S.

weakest_evidence_point: The cited dash row at depth 12/27 confirms an I2S contribution somewhere in the ancestry, but the leaf mutation_op chain is pure havoc, so we cannot prove the specific byte that flipped aIpProto away from kProtoUdp was the I2S substitution rather than later havoc on an I2S-seeded ancestor; additionally the upstream extension-header parsing means the byte that determines aIpProto at L937 is not necessarily at IPv6 header offset 6.
